source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A square based pyramid (that is symmetrical) is standing on top of a cube with side length $\ell=10 \mathrm{~cm}$ such that their square faces perfectly line up. The cube is initially standing still on flat ground and both objects have the same uniform density. The coefficient of friction between every surface is the same value of $\mu=0.3$. The cube is then given an initial speed $v$ in some direction parallel to the floor. What is the maximum possible value of $v$ such that the base of the pyramid will always remain parallel to the top of the cube? Answer in meters per second.

<image_1>
extraction_notes: Answer field is a choice letter and was mapped to the corresponding option text.
image_1 contains a PIL image object placeholder rather than a path/URL, so no image path was extracted.

image_dependency: question_text fully specifies the geometry in words (cube, square-based symmetric pyramid, aligned square faces) without needing a diagram
although an image exists in asset_registry_record.image_manifest, the setup appears solvable from the text alone
multi_step: the problem combines geometry and mechanics: stacked bodies, uniform density, friction, and motion on the floor
it asks for a maximum allowable initial speed subject to a stability/orientation constraint ('base of the pyramid will always remain parallel')
multiple conditions must be integrated, including cube size, shared density statement, and coefficient of friction across interfaces
this is not a direct lookup; it likely requires chained reasoning about forces/acceleration and rotational stability
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and the prompt specifies the target unit as meters per second
answer_text is a single concrete value ('1.07'), which is easy to compare against a solver or checker with tolerance
multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional coarse verification signal
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

risk_flags: pyramid height is not explicitly stated in the question text, which may create modeling ambiguity
the phrase 'given an initial speed v' may leave some ambiguity about the exact dynamical interpretation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, and the registry passed with no recorded asset issues
the sample has high multi-step and verifiability scores, indicating good candidate value
the problem appears text-dominant and solvable from the question text even without relying on the image
risk flags indicate some modeling ambiguity, but the core semantics are still recoverable and well supported by the available assets
